Pine tree exhibits significant soil erosion at base, resulting in exposure of the structural root system. Current lean is minimal but there is clear evidence of ongoing soil loss, which will continue to compromise integrity of root plate. Progressive erosion is expected to further destabilize tree, increasing likelihood of failure. Given the tree’s size and condition, this presents a foreseeable risk of structural failure.  Visible root exposure and soil displacement indicate partial loss of lateral support has already occurred. Tree is located in close proximity to two residences, placing structures and occupants within the potential fall zone. Next door neighbor reports tree sways dangerously during high winds. Neighbor reports existing fear of tree failure. Based on these conditions, the tree meets the criteria for hazardous or dying classification due to compromised structural stability and risk of failure.Reason for Removal:Dying treeComments:Pine tree exhibits significant soil erosion at base, resulting in exposure of the structural root system.
